title: Ryan M. Freel | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
Fragomen in Chicago, IL
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
333 W Wacker Dr 15th Floor
Fragomen in Chicago, IL
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
333 W Wacker Dr 15th Floor
Fragomen in Chicago, IL
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
333 W Wacker Dr 15th Floor
Ryan helps multinational Fortune 500 companies build custom immigration programs across multiple global jurisdictions. He is skilled in helping clients move worldwide workforces quickly and efficiently, while ensuring compliance with local laws and regulations. Additionally, he works closely with clients to help set up their electronic systems for greater efficiency, using APIs to manage risks and compliance.
Counseling companies across several industries, Ryan focuses on the energy, tech, defense and relocation sectors. He is also experienced in M&A/divestiture projects with an immigration component, helping leadership execute on immediate compliance and minimize loss of productivity.
Specific to the energy industry, clients rely on Ryan’s keen insight on the challenges related to a rotational workforce in remote locations and international waters, and determining the necessary offshore compliance. In 2014, Ryan was seconded to a well-known oil field services company as the Immigration Program Lead. In this capacity, Ryan successfully implemented and co-managed a comprehensive global immigration program.
Serving as the head of the firm’s Houston office, Ryan uses his 20-plus years of experience in various roles within the firm, including posts overseas in the Asia Pacific region, to help clients solve their most complex immigration and global mobility challenges.
Additionally, Ryan looks to his own passion for travel and learning about new cultures, and experience of living abroad—both personally and professionally—to help him inform his work and how he relates to his clients’ needs. Having worked in-house for a period of years, Ryan also has a deep understanding of the priorities from that side of a case. He uses this experience to help clients navigate each part of the immigration process, providing the answers to the questions that matter to everyone involved, and ultimately working together to achieve a successful result.
Clients appreciate Ryan’s transparent and proactive approach and his dedication to making an impact on their immigration programs. His client philosophy has resulted in many long-term relationships and continued collaboration with companies in the U.S. and abroad.
Ryan’s commitment to building relationships also extends to the firm where he is known for his mentorship. He strongly believes in helping young lawyers in their career progression but also learning from them and applying what he has learned to his own practice. Active in the community, Ryan often looks for ways to partner with Houston-area organizations where he and the entire office can donate their time and help, further fostering a culture of togetherness and mentorship.

- University of Miami School of Law, J.D., 1999
- Salve Regina University, B.A., cum laude , 1996
Bar Admissions: Texas, New York
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Outstanding Mentor Award (Houston Young Lawyers Association), 2022-2023
Listed in Best Lawyers in America (Woodward/White, Inc.), 2024-2026
Listed in Chambers USA (Chambers and Partners) , 2021-2025

### Professional accomplishments
Member, Australian-American Chamber of Commence
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association
Member, Brazil-Texas Chamber of Commerce
Board of Directors, Houston Relocation Professionals, 2025-2028
Member, Houston Relocation Professionals
Member, New York State Bar Association
Member, Society for Human Resource Management
Member, Texas Bar Association
Former Member, Employee Relocation Council (GMS Certified)
Former Member, Singapore International Chamber of Commerce
Former Member, American Bar Association
Following a sale of a technology company, managed all the immigration compliance aspects across seven countries during the COVID-19 pandemic, navigating abrupt changes in regulations, border closures, entry restrictions and quarantine requirements for a large number of affected employees.
Managed immigration compliance aspects of a joint venture transaction for an offshore drilling company and provided strategic guidance to help assess the immigration consequences of this transaction on the workforce and ensure minimal disruption to the organization.
Played a critical role in the development of a Fortune 100 company’s Remote Work Policy, which enabled the client to keep its business operating and its employees safe and happy, while complying with evolving processes and regulations.
Provided senior account management leadership for a large multinational IT company and a global oil field services company by establishing global best practices, refining the companies’ global immigration programs and improving operational efficiencies and process improvements to minimize client expenditures.
Led the commercial management of the firm’s Global Co-Counsel Network and established policies for the qualification, selection and ongoing management of the firm’s immigration service providers.
Managed the inception and ongoing operational development of Fragomen’s Singapore and South Africa offices.
Served as Fragomen’s primary point of contact in the establishment and execution of a joint venture between Fragomen and Abacus Consulting in the United Arab Emirates.
Researched and drafted a comprehensive best practices report analyzing five Fortune 500 companies with regard to compliance management of business travelers, immigration program management, use of technology, tracking and monitoring of expiration dates, and internal immigration policies.

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Staying the Course: Navigating Today’s Immigration Landscape with Confidence and Compliance.” Texas SHRM Global Business Conference , Austin, TX, 18 September 2025.
Speaker, “Corporate Roundtable: Be Aware and Prepared: Immigration Compliance and Audit Readiness.” 2025 Forum for Expatriate Management Americas Summit , Dallas, TX, 15 May 2025.
Speaker, “Accelerate Immigration Assessment for the Offshore energy and Maritime Industries.” 2023 Texas SHRM Global HR Conference , Houston, Texas, 14 September 2023.
Panelist, “Global Mobility and Immigration.” 2023 St. Louis Employee Relocation Council Conference , St. Louis, Missouri, 16 August 2023.
Panelist, “Making the Case: Advocating for International Visa and Immigration Compliance in Your Travel Program.” Global Business Travel Association National Conference , Dallas, Texas, 15 August 2023.
Speaker, “Untapped Talent Pools: Leveraging Digital Nomad Visas in Your Program.” 2023 Gulf Coast Symposium on HR Issues , Houston, Texas, 13 July 2023.
Panelist, “A Focus on Latin America.” Southeastern Regional Relocation Council Spring Event , Fort Lauderdale, Florida. 11 May 2023.
Speaker, “Crisis Management for the Unexpected in Global Mobility.” On The Move with US Bank , Podcast, 11 April 2023.
Speaker, “Global Mobility Executive Houston Roundtable.” Global Mobility Executive Leaders Exchange , Houston, Texas, 30 March 2023.
Panelist, “Crisis Management: Lessons Learned in Immigration and Mobility.“ Cartus , Webinar, 21 March 2023.
Co-author, “White Paper: Immigration in the Offshore Energy and Maritime Industry.” Fragomen , 15 March 2023.
Speaker, “Global Mobility and Relocation Round-Up.” Houston Relocation Professional Conference , Houston, Texas, 8 March 2023.
Speaker, “Has The Mobility Industry Opened Pandora’s Suitcase? Part II - Business Travel Immigration Compliance.” Minnesota Employee Relocation Council , St. Paul, Minnesota, 13 July 2021.
Co-author, “Cost Savings in the Modern Talent and Mobility World.” Fragomen Whitepaper .
Co-author, “Developing Business Relationships in the Global Immigration Sector.” American Immigration Lawyers Association .
Ryan has served as the firm’s primary speaker on all matters pertaining to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. He also lectures on global immigration compliance and best practices to Fortune 500 companies on a regular basis.
In addition, Ryan is a speaker and presenter on global immigration law for the following organizations:
- American Council on International Personnel
- Association of Corporate Counsel
- California Bar Association
- Corporate Relocation Council
- Council for Global Immigration
- NAFSA (Association of International Educators)
- Society for Human Resource Management

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
